channel.title: Drupal AI
channel.description: Stay informed about developments in Drupal AI.

item.title: #3545462: Move the input of structured output to an object.
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 1.3.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Diff: 7d256a9 · +790/-28 prod · +266/-6 test · 10 files
  • Discussion: #3545462 · 10 contributors · 57 comments

Summary

The AI module now provides a dedicated data transfer object (DTO) to help developers request structured JSON responses from AI providers. Previously, developers passed raw arrays to define the shape of AI responses. This worked but had no validation and different providers required different formats. The new DTO validates schema names, checks that required fields are present, and normalizes the array format for provider compatibility. Developers can still pass plain arrays for backwards compatibility, but the DTO approach is now recommended for cleaner code and better error messages. This lays the groundwork for future features like automated schema generation from PHP classes.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • Deprecation: ChatInput::setChatStrictSchema() and getChatStrictSchema() deprecated in ai:1.3.0, removed in ai:2.0.0; set strict on StructuredOutputSchema instead
  • New API: New StructuredOutputSchema DTO class for structured output requests (recommended; plain arrays still accepted for backwards compatibility)

Technical details

The new StructuredOutputSchema class provides a validated wrapper for structured output requests. It accepts four properties: name (validated as lowercase letters, numbers, underscores, hyphens only), description (optional), strict (boolean, default false), and json_schema (the actual JSON schema array, must contain a 'properties' key). The class uses Symfony Validator constraints on public properties to validate inputs at construction or when set via setters.

The DTO can be instantiated directly or created from an array via fromArray(). The toArray() method returns a normalized array with the key 'schema' for the schema content, matching the documented format most providers expect. For backwards compatibility, both the DTO and ChatInput::setChatStructuredJsonSchema() accept either 'schema' or 'json_schema' as the key for the schema content, but normalize and store it as 'schema'.

When a StructuredOutputSchema object is passed to ChatInput::setChatStructuredJsonSchema(), it is converted to the normalized array format and stored. For backwards compatibility, the strict flag is also copied to the now-deprecated chatStrictSchema property. Plain arrays are still accepted and are normalized to the same format. The API Explorer was updated to use the new DTO when processing JSON schema form input.

Upgrade

For module developers using structured output:

The recommended approach is now to use the StructuredOutputSchema DTO:

use Drupal\ai\Dto\StructuredOutputSchema;

$schema = new StructuredOutputSchema(
  name: 'weather_response',
  description: 'Structured weather data',
  strict: TRUE,
  json_schema: [
    'properties' => [
      'temperature' => ['type' => 'number'],
      'location' => ['type' => 'string'],
    ],
  ],
);

$input->setChatStructuredJsonSchema($schema);

Plain arrays still work but will be normalized (the 'json_schema' key is accepted but stored as 'schema'):

$input->setChatStructuredJsonSchema([
  'name' => 'my_schema',
  'strict' => TRUE,
  'schema' => [
    'properties' => ['field' => ['type' => 'string']],
  ],
]);

Replace calls to setChatStrictSchema() and getChatStrictSchema() with the strict property on the schema object or array (these methods are deprecated in ai:1.3.0 and removed in ai:2.0.0).

Timeline

  • Opened: September 8, 2025
  • Committed: February 26, 2026 (5 months later)

Collaboration

abhisekmazumdar led the implementation work, iterating through multiple rounds of feedback to address backwards compatibility concerns, schema key normalization, and documentation. b_sharpe performed a thorough review and managed a complex merge request cleanup when the fork tracking broke. a.dmitriiev contributed rebase work and caught deprecation message issues. marcus_johansson identified a critical regression related to the schema key format during testing and guided the normalization strategy to maintain provider compatibility. The issue surfaced important architectural discussions about validation scope and provider abstraction.

Lessons learned

  • When building an abstraction layer for multiple providers, accept both structured objects and raw arrays at the API boundary, then normalize to a standard format internally. This allows gradual migration to the structured approach while maintaining backward compatibility with existing array-based implementations. (via marcus_johansson, abhisekmazumdar)
  • When different providers require different input formats for the same concept, create a translation layer in each provider module rather than exposing provider-specific variations in the shared API. The abstraction should enforce a common interface that works across providers, allowing users to switch providers without code changes. (via marcus_johansson)
  • When introducing structured DTOs to replace arrays, support multiple key names (like 'schema' and 'json_schema') during the transition period. Normalize to a canonical key internally while accepting legacy formats on input to prevent breaking production code. (via abhisekmazumdar)

This summary is AI-generated and may contain errors. Check the original issue on Drupal.org for the full picture. For more digests, see Drupal Digests.

item.title: #3569770: Add tooltip component to AI Module for use in AI Dashboard using native Popover API
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 1.3.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Diff: 7bf7abc · +190/-4 prod · +5/-0 infra · 12 files
  • Discussion: #3569770 · 5 contributors · 17 comments

Summary

The AI module now includes a reusable tooltip component built on the native HTML Popover API with CSS Anchor Positioning. Tooltips automatically reposition themselves when near screen edges, improving usability on smaller viewports. The first implementation appears in the AI settings form, where information icons now display helpful tooltips on hover or keyboard focus instead of basic browser tooltips. This provides a better user experience with consistent styling and intelligent positioning across the module.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The tooltip component uses the HTML Popover API with popover="hint" and CSS Anchor Positioning with position-try-fallbacks: flip-inline to automatically flip tooltips when they would extend beyond the viewport edge. The implementation lives in ai_global.js as a Drupal behavior that finds elements with a data-ai-tooltip attribute and creates associated popover elements.

The JavaScript handles delayed showing (500ms) and immediate hiding with timer management to prevent race conditions. It attaches both mouse and keyboard event handlers for accessibility. The ai_global library is now attached globally via hook_page_attachments() instead of only on forms, enabling tooltips anywhere in the module. All classes use the ai- prefix to avoid conflicts.

The native Popover API is supported by all major browsers since 2025. The hint value provides additional tooltip priority features but degrades gracefully in browsers that don't support it. No polyfill was added since core functionality works universally. The CSS uses Gin admin theme variables for consistent styling with borders, shadows, and proper spacing.

Timeline

  • Opened: January 27, 2026
  • Committed: February 26, 2026 (1 month later)

Collaboration

bbruno implemented the tooltip component and provided thorough testing instructions with a visual demonstration. ahmad khader performed comprehensive manual testing across multiple scenarios including viewport positioning, keyboard navigation, RTL support, and accessibility attributes, providing detailed verification of each requirement.

Lessons learned

  • When adding UI components that may be reused across a module, use data attributes (e.g., data-*) to declaratively attach functionality to elements. This separates configuration from implementation and allows developers to add features like tooltips without writing JavaScript, making the component API simple and intuitive for other developers to adopt. (via bbruno, ahmad khader)
  • When building tooltip or popover components, leverage native browser APIs (like the Popover API) even when optional features (like popover='hint') aren't universally supported. If the core functionality works across all major browsers and the unsupported parts provide only minor enhancements (like tooltip priority), the simplicity of avoiding polyfills outweighs the loss of those features. (via bbruno)

This summary is AI-generated and may contain errors. Check the original issue on Drupal.org for the full picture. For more digests, see Drupal Digests.

item.title: #3518356: Add Full screen and Expand options to Deepchat chatbot
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 2.0.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Diff: 16f7a56 · +500/-24 · 20 files
  • Discussion: #3518356 · 12 contributors · 36 comments

Summary

The AI Chatbot block now offers configurable expansion modes to improve readability when the chatbot is placed in the toolbar. Administrators can choose between three options: none, expand (widens the sidebar and shifts page content), or fullscreen (opens as a modal overlay). This addresses usability concerns with the default sidebar's limited space, giving users more room to interact with longer conversations. The feature includes smooth animations, keyboard support for exiting fullscreen with the Escape key, and conditional loading of assets to avoid unnecessary overhead.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

When the AI Chatbot block is configured with toolbar placement, a new "Expansion method" select field appears in the block settings under styling options. The field offers three values: "none", "expand", and "fullscreen". The expand mode uses CSS custom properties (--toolbar-chatbot-expand-amount) to dynamically calculate width adjustments, pushing Drupal's displacement offset and page content to the right by 20rem. The fullscreen mode applies a fade-in animation and renders the chatbot as a modal using pure CSS (fixed positioning with a backdrop overlay), preserving the chatbot's current state without creating a new dialog element. The fullscreen library (toolbar-fullscreen.css) is conditionally attached only when the fullscreen method is selected. JavaScript in toolbar-chatbot.js handles expand button clicks, toggling body classes (ai-chatbot-expanded, ai-chatbot-fullscreen), managing aria-expanded attributes for accessibility, and listening for the Escape key to exit fullscreen. Exit animations use animationend events with double requestAnimationFrame to ensure clean state transitions. Both expansion states reset properly when the chatbot is closed. The expand button is omitted from the DOM when set to "none". SVG icons are updated to use currentColor for theme consistency.

Timeline

  • Opened: April 10, 2025
  • First commit: February 19, 2026 (10 months later)
  • Last commit: February 26, 2026 (6 days later)

Collaboration

bbruno implemented the feature, including the block configuration, CSS architecture, JavaScript state management, and accessibility support. petar_basic provided two rounds of review with detailed suggestions that improved code quality. ahmad khader conducted thorough testing across multiple scenarios, identified edge cases around state cleanup, and provided actionable feedback on code consistency and accessibility attributes. rajeshreeputra and davidbp made their first commits to the issue fork, contributing to the collaborative effort.

This summary is AI-generated and may contain errors. Check the original issue on Drupal.org for the full picture. For more digests, see Drupal Digests.

item.title: #3575631: Add a summarize automator
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.3.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Diff: e44d484 · +222/-0 prod · +3/-0 infra · 7 files
  • Discussion: #3575631 · 3 contributors · 10 comments

Summary

The AI module can now automatically generate summaries from long text fields. When editing content, you can configure a field to automatically create a condensed summary from another field, like creating a teaser from a body field. This uses specialized AI summarization models rather than chat-based AI, providing faster and more focused summaries. The feature works with providers like Hugging Face and Google Chrome's built-in summarization. This is useful for creating article previews, meta descriptions, search snippets, or newsletter excerpts without manual writing.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

This adds two new automator plugins for the summarize operation type introduced in #3574178. The LlmSummarizeToStringLong plugin targets string_long fields, while LlmSummarizeToTextLong targets text_long fields with text format support.

Both plugins extend a new Summarize base class that implements the core summarization logic. Unlike chat-based automators, this uses the AI module's summarize endpoint with dedicated summarization models. The base class overrides needsPrompt() and advancedMode() to return FALSE since summarization doesn't require custom prompts.

The generate() method iterates through base field items, creating a SummarizationInput object for each, and calls the provider's summarize() method. For text_long fields, the plugin adds a form field to select the output text format, ensuring proper format handling for formatted text fields.

Documentation was added covering supported providers (Hugging Face models like facebook/bart-large-cnn, sshleifer/distilbart-cnn-12-6, google/pegasus-xsum, and Google Chrome) and eight common use cases ranging from article summaries to moderation review snippets.

Timeline

  • Opened: February 25, 2026
  • Committed: February 26, 2026

Collaboration

abhisekmazumdar provided thorough functional testing across multiple field types and edge cases, verifying behavior with empty base fields, different text formats, and documentation accuracy. The review also identified future considerations for text_with_summary field support.

This summary is AI-generated and may contain errors. Check the original issue on Drupal.org for the full picture. For more digests, see Drupal Digests.

item.title: #3575626: Unsupported parameter: 'top_p' is not supported with this model.
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Major bug report
  • Version: 1.3.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Diff: e3a230a · +6/-0 · 1 file
  • Discussion: #3575626 · 4 contributors · 11 comments

Summary

The OpenAI provider module was sending configuration parameters to GPT-5.x and o-series models that those models do not support, causing API requests to fail with an error. The module now automatically removes unsupported parameters like top_p, temperature, frequency_penalty, and presence_penalty when using these newer model families. This allows sites to successfully use GPT-5.1, GPT-5.2, and o-series models without manual configuration changes.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The OpenAiProvider plugin sends various configuration parameters to the OpenAI API based on user settings and defaults. These include sampling parameters like top_p (nucleus sampling), temperature (randomness), frequency_penalty, and presence_penalty (repetition control).

GPT-5.x models and o-series models have different API constraints than earlier GPT models. They reject requests that include these sampling parameters, even though some API documentation incorrectly marks them as optional. When these parameters were sent, the API returned an error: "Unsupported parameter: 'top_p' is not supported with this model."

The fix adds logic in the OpenAiProvider::chat() method to detect when a GPT-5.x or o-series model is being used by checking the model ID prefix. For these models, the code now explicitly removes all four sampling parameters from the configuration array before sending the API request. This is applied in the same conditional block that already handles converting max_tokens to max_completion_tokens for these newer models.

Timeline

  • Opened: February 25, 2026
  • Committed: February 25, 2026

Collaboration

marcus_johansson quickly identified the root cause by cross-referencing the API documentation with actual model behavior, discovering that the documentation was incorrect about which parameters are optional. They raised the priority appropriately and provided a fix that handles both the GPT-5.x and o-series model families. siva01 provided clear error logs and linked to model documentation, making it easy to diagnose the issue.

This summary is AI-generated and may contain errors. Check the original issue on Drupal.org for the full picture. For more digests, see Drupal Digests.

item.title: #3574182: Add support for Summarize
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.0.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Diff: 2d02b4c · +46/-8 · 3 files
  • Discussion: #3574182 · 3 contributors · 7 comments

Summary

The Huggingface AI provider now supports text summarization. Sites using Huggingface models can now automatically generate summaries of longer text content. This matches the new summarization operation type added to the AI module, allowing consistent summarization features across different AI providers. The feature works through Huggingface's summarization inference endpoints.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The HuggingfaceProvider class now implements the SummarizationInterface from the AI module core. The new summarize() method accepts text input (either as a string, array, or SummarizationInput object), validates that a Huggingface endpoint is configured for the model, and calls the API's summarization endpoint.

The implementation normalizes different input formats into a consistent array with text and prompt keys. It validates that input text is not empty before making the API call. The response is decoded from JSON and validated to ensure it contains the expected summary_text field in the first array element. Results are wrapped in a SummarizationOutput object.

The provider's supported operations list and model filters were updated to include 'summarize' alongside existing operations like chat, embeddings, and image classification. A minor fix was also included to explicitly set the content type for image classification requests.

Timeline

  • Opened: February 18, 2026
  • Committed: February 25, 2026 (7 days later)

Collaboration

Marcus Johansson created and implemented this feature as part of broader summarization support across the AI module ecosystem. Abhisekmazumdar reviewed and tested the implementation alongside the related summarization explorer task, confirming it was ready to merge.

This summary is AI-generated and may contain errors. Check the original issue on Drupal.org for the full picture. For more digests, see Drupal Digests.

item.title: #3574178: Add a new summarize operation type and explorer
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.3.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Diff: b1081a5 · +842/-0 · 8 files
  • Discussion: #3574178 · 3 contributors · 12 comments

Summary

The AI module now supports dedicated text summarization models. Some AI models are specifically trained for summarization tasks and can run more efficiently than general-purpose chat models. This matters for sites using on-device models like Chrome's built-in AI or specialized Huggingface and Ollama summarization models. A new explorer interface lets site builders test summarization with different providers and models before deploying them in production features.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The module introduces a new summarize operation type alongside existing operations like chat and text generation. The architecture follows the established operation type pattern with three components: SummarizationInterface for providers to implement, SummarizationInput for text plus optional guidance prompts, and SummarizationOutput for the resulting summary.

The SummarizationGenerator explorer plugin provides a testing interface at /admin/config/ai/explorers/summarize_generator. Users enter text to summarize, optionally add instructions like "summarize in bullet points," and select a provider and model. The explorer handles empty input validation, configuration errors, and API exceptions with clear error messages.

The input object accepts an optional prompt parameter because some summarization models support guidance while others do not. This flexibility accommodates both specialized single-purpose models and general LLMs that implement summarization. The output normalizes to a plain string summary, consistent with other operation types.

Providers like Huggingface can now offer summarization-specific models (such as facebook/bart-large-cnn) separately from their chat models. This enables sites to use smaller, faster, or more accurate models for summarization tasks.

Timeline

  • Opened: February 18, 2026
  • Committed: February 25, 2026 (7 days later)

Collaboration

marcus_johansson authored the new summarization operation type and explorer interface. abhisekmazumdar tested the feature with the Huggingface provider integration and verified both happy path and error handling before marking it ready to commit. marcus_johansson noted that error feedback should be flagged as needs work rather than treated as nitpicks, helping establish quality standards for the issue queue.

This summary is AI-generated and may contain errors. Check the original issue on Drupal.org for the full picture. For more digests, see Drupal Digests.

item.title: #3570609: Add support for Object Detection operation
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.3.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Diff: 89ef0e1 · +1244/-6 prod · +2/-0 infra · 20 files
  • Discussion: #3570609 · 4 contributors · 20 comments

Summary

The AI module now supports object detection, a computer vision capability that identifies objects within images and returns their labels, confidence scores, and bounding box coordinates. This enables new use cases like automated image cropping, visual search, and content analysis. Providers that support object detection (like Huggingface with models such as facebook/detr-resnet-50) can now be used through the standardized AI module interface. The optional AI API Explorer submodule includes a testing interface for experimenting with object detection calls.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New object_detection operation type for detecting objects in images with bounding boxes and confidence scores

Technical details

This feature adds a complete operation type for object detection following the AI module's established patterns. The implementation includes ObjectDetectionInterface with an objectDetection() method, ObjectDetectionInput that wraps an ImageFile, and ObjectDetectionOutput that returns an array of ObjectDetectionItem objects. Each item contains a label (e.g. "person", "car"), an optional confidence score (0.0 to 1.0), and a bounding box array with xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax coordinates in pixels.

The API Explorer plugin ObjectDetectionGenerator provides a form-based interface where users can upload images and see detected objects rendered in a table. The implementation handles errors gracefully, including missing models, configuration issues, and empty results. Documentation follows the module's pattern with a dedicated page explaining the interface, showing code examples with the Huggingface provider, and linking to the relevant PHP classes. The provider matrix was updated to indicate Huggingface supports this operation.

Upgrade

Module developers can now implement object detection by having their AI provider plugin implement ObjectDetectionInterface and define the objectDetection() method:

use Drupal\ai\OperationType\GenericType\ImageFile;
use Drupal\ai\OperationType\ObjectDetection\ObjectDetectionInput;

$image_binary = file_get_contents('photo.jpg');
$normalized_file = new ImageFile($image_binary, 'image/jpeg', 'photo.jpg');
$input = new ObjectDetectionInput($normalized_file);

$detection_items = \Drupal::service('ai.provider')
  ->createInstance('huggingface')
  ->objectDetection($input, 'facebook/detr-resnet-50', ['my-module'])
  ->getNormalized();

foreach ($detection_items as $item) {
  $label = $item->getLabel();
  $score = $item->getConfidenceScore();
  $box = $item->getBox(); // ['xmin' => 535, 'ymin' => 1332, ...]
}

See the Object Detection documentation for complete details.

Timeline

  • Opened: January 30, 2026
  • Committed: February 23, 2026 (23 days later)

Collaboration

a.dmitriiev authored the feature and persisted through test failures caused by unrelated merge conflicts from #3570097. marcus_johansson tested the implementation with the Huggingface provider integration in #3570608, verified it worked correctly, and added documentation before merging. fago provided review feedback that caught the test failures.

This summary is AI-generated and may contain errors. Check the original issue on Drupal.org for the full picture. For more digests, see Drupal Digests.

item.title: #3571925: Output streaming is handled inconsistently, fails to work in API AI-explorer
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Major bug report
  • Version: 1.3.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3571925 · 4 contributors · 13 comments

Summary

Output streaming for AI responses was failing in the API AI Explorer and handled inconsistently across different AI module integrations when using web servers like nginx. The four different places where streaming responses were generated each set different HTTP headers or none at all, causing streaming to fail in some contexts. A new centralized AiStreamedResponse class now ensures all streaming responses include the necessary headers to work reliably across different server configurations and integrations.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The AI module generates streaming responses in four locations: CKEditor integration, DeepChat API, ChatForm, and API Explorer. Each implemented streaming differently with inconsistent HTTP headers. Some set X-Accel-Buffering: no for nginx compatibility, others did not. Cache control headers varied between no-cache,must-revalidate, no-cache, or missing entirely. Content-type headers were also inconsistent, with some incorrectly setting text/event-stream when not actually using Server-Sent Events.

The fix introduces a centralized AiStreamedResponse class that extends Symfony's StreamedResponse. This class sets all required headers by default: X-Accel-Buffering: no for nginx/Apache streaming support, Cache-Control: no-cache to prevent response caching, and appropriate content-type headers. All four streaming locations now use this unified class, ensuring consistent behavior across all AI integrations. The X-Accel-Buffering header was the critical missing piece that prevented streaming from working in nginx environments, which is why API Explorer failed while CKEditor worked.

Timeline

  • Opened: February 6, 2026

Collaboration

fago identified the inconsistency across multiple streaming implementations and created a comprehensive analysis table documenting the differences. petar_basic tested the fix with non-agent chatbots to verify streaming functionality worked correctly across different integration points.

This summary is AI-generated and may contain errors. Check the original issue on Drupal.org for the full picture. For more digests, see Drupal Digests.

item.title: #3570252: Cannot select all models for "Chat with Complex JSON"
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Major bug report
  • Version: 1.2.0
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3570252 · 5 contributors · 12 comments

Summary

Site administrators can now select GPT-5 models when configuring "Chat with Complex JSON" in the AI module's default settings. Previously, only older GPT-3 and GPT-4 models appeared in the dropdown for structured JSON output, even though GPT-5 models support this feature. The limitation was caused by filtering logic that excluded newer model naming patterns.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The AI Provider OpenAI module filters available models for the "Chat with Complex JSON" operation type based on model name patterns. This filtering ensures only models that support structured JSON output appear in the configuration form at /admin/config/ai/settings.

The original filtering logic did not include GPT-5 model name patterns, so these newer models were excluded from the dropdown even though OpenAI documents them as supporting structured output. The fix adds GPT-5 patterns to the allowlist so administrators can select models like gpt-5.1 and gpt-5.2 for JSON-structured responses.

OpenAI's documentation confirms that GPT-5.1 and GPT-5.2 models support structured output functionality, making them suitable for chat operations that require complex JSON responses. This change aligns the module's model filtering with OpenAI's current model capabilities.

Timeline

  • Opened: January 29, 2026

Collaboration

Akhil babu identified the model name filtering issue and proposed the solution. Marcus_johansson confirmed that GPT-5 models support structured output by referencing OpenAI's documentation and connected this work to related improvements in #3572299. Breidert raised important questions about validation and testing to ensure the fix worked correctly.

This summary is AI-generated and may contain errors. Check the original issue on Drupal.org for the full picture. For more digests, see Drupal Digests.

item.title: #3571220: Adjust Global AI CSS and Introduce new Icons
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 1.2.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Diff: 9bd9728 · +215/-76 · 13 files
  • Discussion: #3571220 · 4 contributors · 18 comments

Summary

The AI module now provides a more flexible and reusable CSS system for styling AI-related interfaces. CSS classes now reference AI-specific variables instead of directly referencing theme variables, making it easier to customize AI components without modifying markup. The update also adds new icon variations for common AI interface elements like settings, status indicators, and external links, giving module developers a consistent visual language across AI features.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

This change introduces a new layer of CSS custom properties prefixed with --ai- that wrap Drupal core's CSS variables. Instead of classes like .ai-font-size-s directly referencing var(--font-size-s), they now reference --ai-font-size-s, which itself falls back to the core variable. This indirection allows developers to override AI-specific styling by targeting the AI variables without needing to modify markup or create custom classes.

The icon system was refactored from modifier classes like .ai-info-link--provider to a BEM-style pattern using .ai-icon-button as the base component and .ai-icon--provider as the modifier. The .ai-icon class can be used standalone for inline icons, while .ai-icon-button provides a full clickable button treatment with hover, focus, and active states. Icon sizing now uses --ai-icon-size and --ai-icon-size-lg variables.

Form-specific styles were extracted from ai_global.css into a new ai_settings_form.css file with its own library (ai_settings_form), keeping the global utilities truly reusable. Nine new icon SVGs were added including settings (gear), status indicators (checkmark-circle, x-circle, warning), and UI elements (external, option, arrow-left-up, cloud-arrow-down, file).

The AiSettingsForm class was updated to use the new icon class names and attach the new settings form library.

Timeline

  • Opened: February 3, 2026
  • Committed: February 20, 2026 (17 days later)

Collaboration

bbruno identified the limitation while implementing the global classes on the AI dashboard and created the merge request with the proposed solution. erichomanchuk made the icon naming more generic and refactored form-specific styles into their own file, establishing cleaner architectural boundaries. danrod rebased the merge request and verified the visual changes worked correctly across the interface.

This summary is AI-generated and may contain errors. Check the original issue on Drupal.org for the full picture. For more digests, see Drupal Digests.

item.title: #3565750: Make it easier to drag and drop agents and tools onto nodes
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Major task
  • Version: 1.0.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3565750 · 4 contributors · 10 comments

Summary

This issue improves the drag-and-drop interface for building AI agent workflows. Previously, connecting agents, tools, assistants, and chatbots required multiple clicks to draw connection lines. Now you can drag components directly onto compatible targets to automatically create connections, making it faster to assemble AI workflows visually.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

This issue addressed usability challenges in the flowdrop_ui_agents module's visual workflow builder. The original interface required users to manually draw connection lines between components (agents, tools, assistants, and chatbots) using multiple clicks.

The fix implements automatic "snap and attach" behavior when dragging components onto compatible targets. The connection rules enforce the module's architecture: tools attach to agents but not assistants, agents attach to assistants or other agents, chatbots attach only to agents, and assistants cannot attach to anything. Tools can also be placed independently on the canvas and later dragged onto compatible targets.

The implementation was completed during a migration to a newer version of the FlowDrop UI library, as referenced in issue #3572310. A regression was noted during development but was ultimately resolved with an improved approach to the drag-and-drop interaction.

Timeline

  • Opened: January 5, 2026

Collaboration

d34dman identified that the fix was delivered through the FlowDrop UI migration work. yautja_cetanu tracked the issue through multiple iterations, caught a regression during demos, and filed follow-up issue #3573955 to address remaining edge cases with dragging tools onto assistants. gxleano confirmed the implementation approach.

This summary is AI-generated and may contain errors. Check the original issue on Drupal.org for the full picture. For more digests, see Drupal Digests.

item.title: #3567144: Chat configuration select lists use machine names not labels
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Major task
  • Version: 1.0.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3567144 · 4 contributors · 10 comments

Summary

This fix corrects configuration forms in the Chatbot feature to display human-readable labels instead of machine names in dropdown lists. Previously, dropdowns for theme selection, region placement, styling options, and toggle states showed internal machine names, making them difficult to read and use. The forms now display proper labels, improving usability for site administrators configuring chatbot appearance and behavior.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

This was a regression in the Chatbot configuration interface where multiple select lists displayed machine names instead of user-friendly labels. The affected fields included theme selection, region placement, style options, placement settings, and toggle state controls in the block placement, styling settings, and advanced settings sections.

The fix was implemented as part of migrating to the latest version of FlowDrop UI in issue #3572310. The migration ensured that form element options properly map machine names to their corresponding labels for display purposes.

No diff is available for this issue as the changes were integrated through the FlowDrop UI Agents project update rather than committed directly to this issue.

Timeline

  • Opened: January 12, 2026

Collaboration

yautja_cetanu identified and reported the regression with multiple affected form fields. d34dman and gxleano coordinated to address the issue as part of the broader FlowDrop UI migration effort, ensuring the fix was integrated systematically rather than patched individually.

This summary is AI-generated and may contain errors. Check the original issue on Drupal.org for the full picture. For more digests, see Drupal Digests.

item.title: #3566304: The Edit Cog for the Chatbot is hidden behind the chat window.
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Major bug report
  • Version: 1.0.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3566304 · 4 contributors · 13 comments

Summary

A visual bug in the chatbot interface prevented users from accessing the edit button. The configuration control (edit cog) was positioned behind the chat window, making it impossible to click. This issue has been resolved as part of a broader update to the FlowDrop UI components.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

This was a CSS z-index or positioning issue in the chatbot user interface where the edit cog control was rendered behind the chat window element. The visual layering problem meant site builders could not access the configuration controls for the chatbot block.

The fix was implemented as part of the migration to the latest version of FlowDrop UI in issue #3572310. No diff is available for this specific issue because the resolution was rolled into the larger UI update. The corrected CSS or layout adjustments ensure the edit controls now appear above the chat window with the proper stacking order.

Timeline

  • Opened: January 8, 2026

Collaboration

yautja_cetanu reported the issue with a screenshot documenting the problem. d34dman and gxleano coordinated to ensure the fix was included in the FlowDrop UI migration work.

This summary is AI-generated and may contain errors. Check the original issue on Drupal.org for the full picture. For more digests, see Drupal Digests.

item.title: #3556181: Create a form element for selecting providers
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 2.0.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Diff: 51d88cc · +2549/-91 prod · +966/-0 test · 18 files
  • Discussion: #3556181 · 9 contributors · 44 comments

Summary

The AI module now provides a standardized form element for selecting AI providers and models. Previously, each module implemented provider selection differently, leading to inconsistent user experiences and duplicated code. The new ai_provider_configuration form element offers a single, reusable component that handles provider and model selection, optional advanced configuration, and default provider support. This streamlines development for AI module contributors and creates a consistent interface for site builders configuring AI features across different modules.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The new AiProviderConfiguration form element (#type => 'ai_provider_configuration') consolidates provider selection logic that was previously spread across the AiProviderFormHelper service and manual implementations using getSimpleProviderModelOptions(). The element accepts an #operation_type property (like chat or text_to_image) and optional configuration properties including #advanced_config (to show provider-specific settings), #default_provider_allowed (to enable a "Default" option), and #inline_description (for contextual help text).

The element uses AJAX to dynamically load provider-specific configuration fields when a provider/model is selected. Configuration fields are generated from the provider's schema via getAvailableConfiguration() and properly type-cast using CastUtility. The element returns a structured array with provider, model, and config keys.

Pseudo operation types are now centralized in a new PseudoOperationTypes utility class. These filtered variants of real operation types (like chat_with_image_vision) apply capability filters while mapping to actual operation types for schema loading. The AiSettingsForm was refactored to use this utility instead of duplicating the definitions.

The element integrates with the existing ai.provider_config schema and the AiProviderInterface::DEFAULT_MODEL_VALUE constant. Comprehensive browser tests in AiProviderConfigurationElementTest verify AJAX loading, default handling, pseudo operation types, and value structure. Developer documentation in ai_provider_configuration_element.md provides usage examples, migration guides from the old patterns, and guidance on saving configuration.

Timeline

  • Opened: November 5, 2025
  • Committed: February 16, 2026 (3 months later)

Collaboration

This feature was developed over several months by abhisekmazumdar, who built the form element and iteratively refined it through extensive testing and review cycles. marcus_johansson provided thorough code reviews, caught edge cases like configuration fields persisting after model changes, and shepherded the issue from concept to completion. breidert contributed a UX improvement by adding the #inline_description property after reviewing UX feedback. emma horrell and angela saldaña provided UX review and suggestions that improved the description placement and overall user experience. jibran conducted code review that helped improve code quality. The issue involved careful collaboration between developers, UX reviewers, and the module maintainer to create a reusable component that will benefit the entire Drupal AI ecosystem.

Lessons learned

  • When building reusable form elements that appear in multiple contexts, allow titles and descriptions to be configurable by implementers rather than hardcoding them. A form element used in different contexts (e.g., selecting models for chat vs. speech-to-text) needs contextual labels to remain meaningful to end users. (via marcus_johansson)
  • When creating form elements with AJAX callbacks that update displayed fields, ensure that field metadata (like descriptions) is preserved after the AJAX response. Field metadata can be lost during AJAX operations if not explicitly handled, leaving users without necessary context. (via abhisekmazumdar)
  • When a form element delegates to a 'default' or 'inherited' option, hide advanced configuration fields for that option. Showing configuration fields when defaults are active creates confusion about whether changes will override system-wide settings. (via marcus_johansson)

This summary is AI-generated and may contain errors. Check the original issue on Drupal.org for the full picture. For more digests, see Drupal Digests.

item.title: #3561969: Research Structured Content/JSON:schema form element
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal plan
  • Version: 1.3.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Diff: ef70da4 · +1582/-18 prod · +386/-0 test · +52/-6 infra · 30 files
  • Discussion: #3561969 · 3 contributors · 20 comments

Summary

The AI module now provides a reusable JSON schema editor for forms. Instead of writing JSON or YAML in a plain textarea, users get a CodeMirror editor with syntax highlighting, line numbers, and real-time validation that shows errors as they type. The editor automatically formats valid JSON and falls back to a plain textarea when JavaScript is unavailable. This makes it easier to configure structured content for AI agents, ECA rules, and API explorers without memorizing JSON syntax rules.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The new ai_json_schema form element wraps a CodeMirror 6 editor configured for JSON. The AiJsonSchema class in src/Element/AiJsonSchema.php implements FormElementBase and generates three HTML elements: a hidden input that carries the form value, a visible textarea for graceful degradation when JavaScript is disabled, and a container div where CodeMirror attaches.

The JavaScript in ui/json-schema-editor/src/ai-json-schema.js uses Drupal behaviors to initialize CodeMirror with basicSetup, json() language support, and jsonParseLinter() for real-time validation. The editor syncs content changes back to the hidden input via an updateListener. All three elements share a unique ID through data attributes so the JavaScript can wire them together.

The editor is built with Vite and uses npm packages codemirror, @codemirror/lang-json, and @codemirror/lint. The build artifacts are excluded from version control but included in tagged releases. The CI pipeline now builds the editor alongside other JavaScript assets in the js_build job. The tag-release.sh script handles versioning and committing the built files for releases.

The ChatGenerator plugin in AI API Explorer now uses #type => 'ai_json_schema' instead of #type => 'textarea' for the structured output field. The test module adds a demonstration form at /admin/config/ai/test-form-elements with a kernel test suite that validates the element structure, data attributes, default values, and form submission behavior.

Timeline

  • Opened: December 8, 2025
  • Committed: February 16, 2026 (2 months later)

Collaboration

Marcus Johansson researched frontend libraries for JSON schema editing, evaluated options, and implemented the CodeMirror-based solution with build tooling, graceful fallback, kernel tests, and comprehensive documentation. They transparently noted which parts were AI-generated versus human-written. A.dmitriiev tested both the API Explorer and test form implementations, verified the editor worked as expected, and updated the GitLab CI configuration to ensure the built JavaScript is available during automated testing.

Lessons learned

  • When building a custom form element with JavaScript enhancement, ensure graceful degradation by providing a functional fallback. If JavaScript fails to load or is disabled, the form element should render as a standard input (like a textarea) so the form remains usable. (via marcus_johansson)
  • When adding JavaScript-enhanced form elements that require build steps, provide clear separation between source code and built artifacts. Exclude large generated files from version control and document the build process so contributors can regenerate the assets locally. (via marcus_johansson)

This summary is AI-generated and may contain errors. Check the original issue on Drupal.org for the full picture. For more digests, see Drupal Digests.

item.title: #3570097: Create a build script for MDXEditor with mentions
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.3.0-beta1
  • Status: Fixed
  • Diff: e7fce84 · +5666/-446 prod · +28/-4 test · +293/-140 infra · 460 files
  • Discussion: #3570097 · 5 contributors · 39 comments

Summary

The AI module now includes a rich text editor with autocomplete support for tokens and custom context variables in prompt fields. This makes it easier to author prompts across the module by providing visual formatting tools and smart suggestions when typing [ for tokens or { for context variables. The editor automatically enhances textareas in prompt forms without breaking older browsers.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The AI module integrated MDXEditor with the @mdxeditor/typeahead-plugin to create a rich prompt authoring experience. A new js_builds/mdxeditor folder contains a Vite-based build pipeline that compiles the editor into mdxeditor/dist. The build is configured in .gitlab-ci.yml to run on every commit (with artifacts cached for tests) but the built files are kept in .gitignore to avoid repository bloat.

When creating a release tag, maintainers must now run scripts/tag-release.sh, which temporarily forces the built files into the commit, creates the tag, then removes them—mirroring the Canvas module's approach. The editor attaches to textareas with specific data-mdxeditor attributes and reads configuration from drupalSettings.mdxeditor, including available tokens and variables defined in each prompt type.

A new Drupal\ai\Hook\FormElement service and ai.libraries.yml entry for mdx_editor load the compiled JavaScript and CSS. The AiPromptSubform service was updated to expose editor configuration, and prompt form fields now render with the MDXEditor library attached. The build process uses Vite for both live reloading during development and production builds.

Timeline

  • Opened: January 28, 2026
  • First commit: January 30, 2026 (2 days later)
  • Last commit: February 16, 2026 (16 days later)

Collaboration

Artem Dmitriev (a.dmitriiev) built the initial MDXEditor integration, exposed the editor configuration to drupalSettings, and fixed test issues. Bruno (bbruno) researched Canvas's build-on-tag approach and helped design the CI/CD workflow for handling the large built files without committing them on every push. Marcus Johansson shepherded the issue, provided the vibe-coded reference implementation, requested follow-up refinements for documentation and asset handling, and made the decision to merge quickly to unblock other work despite remaining follow-ups.

Lessons learned

  • When adding large built JavaScript artifacts to a project, exclude them from version control and build them only during tagged releases. This prevents repository bloat from repeated build changes while ensuring stable releases include all necessary assets. (via marcus_johansson, a.dmitriiev, bbruno)
  • When integrating complex JavaScript components into forms, use data attributes on the original element to configure progressive enhancement. This allows the enhanced UI to fail gracefully to the original form element and enables configuration to be passed via server-side rendering. (via a.dmitriiev)

This summary is AI-generated and may contain errors. Check the original issue on Drupal.org for the full picture. For more digests, see Drupal Digests.

item.title: #3566583: Add Field Widget Actions (FWA) support for text-to-image media generation on the media_library_widget for core Image media
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.3.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Diff: 239ad1f · +526/-8 prod · +504/-0 test · 12 files
  • Discussion: #3566583 · 7 contributors · 33 comments

Summary

Content editors can now generate images using AI while editing content. A new action button on media fields lets editors type a prompt and instantly create image media entities without leaving the edit form. This makes it easier to create hero images, teaser graphics, or illustrations for articles using text-to-image AI models. The feature works with the Media Library widget and requires configuring an AI automator for the field.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

This feature adds a new Field Widget Action plugin TextToImageMediaLibrary that integrates text-to-image generation with the media_library_widget. The plugin extends AutomatorBaseAction and targets entity_reference fields pointing to media entities with image bundles.

The isAvailable() method verifies that the field references media entities and that at least one target bundle uses the image source plugin. The singleElementFormAlter() method hides the action button when the field has reached its cardinality limit to prevent validation errors.

The aiAutomatorsAjax() handler triggers the automator, collects generated media IDs, and uses AJAX commands to update the Media Library widget's hidden selection field and trigger the update button. The saveFormValues() method stores the media IDs in the form array for the AJAX handler to retrieve.

The TextToMediaImage base class was updated to make the media type selection required and moved it to the top of the form (weight 4) with improved description text warning about required fields. Documentation was added showing how to configure the automator in both automatic (on save) and manual (Field Widget Action button) modes.

A kernel test TextToImageMediaFieldWidgetProcessingTest was added covering both the Field Widget Action path (simulating button clicks) and the direct save processing path (automatic generation on entity save).

Timeline

  • Opened: January 9, 2026
  • Committed: February 16, 2026 (1 month later)

Collaboration

Fago shepherded this feature through multiple iterations, adding comprehensive documentation with step-by-step configuration examples, fixing several UX issues discovered during testing (hidden media type field, missing cardinality checks, button visibility), and adding test coverage. A.dmitriiev reviewed thoroughly, requested documentation improvements, and adapted the tests for the 2.0.x branch to follow the new multi-automator structure. Annmarysruthy provided valuable QA testing confirming the feature worked as expected.

Lessons learned

  • When building Field Widget Actions, implement the isAvailable() method to control when the action appears. If the method is missing, the action may appear in inappropriate contexts (like when a single-value field is already full), leading to validation errors when users try to use it. (via fago)
  • When implementing a feature that requires specific configuration (like automators + field widget actions), provide documentation with concrete setup examples showing each configuration step. Users cannot effectively use features without understanding how to wire the components together. (via a.dmitriiev)

This summary is AI-generated and may contain errors. Check the original issue on Drupal.org for the full picture. For more digests, see Drupal Digests.

item.title: #3566576: Add Field Widget Actions (FWA) support for Chart field using the chart_config_default widget
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.3.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Diff: 7bb7827 · +1015/-41 · 22 files
  • Discussion: #3566576 · 6 contributors · 26 comments

Summary

The AI Automators module can now generate chart data for Chart fields when editors click a button in the content form. This lets site builders add "Generate Chart" buttons that use AI to extract structured data from text fields (like a body field) and automatically populate chart configurations. The feature uses the existing Field Widget Actions framework to provide an in-form button for Chart fields using the chart_config_default widget, similar to how FAQ and taxonomy fields already work.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

This feature adds a new Field Widget Action plugin for Chart fields. The Chart plugin in ai_automators/src/Plugin/FieldWidgetAction/Chart.php extends AutomatorBaseAction and declares support for the chart_config_default widget and chart_config field type using PHP attributes. It implements the same pattern as other automator field widget actions like Email and FAQ.

The plugin's aiAutomatorsAjax() method handles AJAX requests when editors click the button, and saveFormValues() populates the chart's data table structure. The populateTableValues() helper method maps AI-generated chart data into the form element structure expected by the Charts module widget, handling both data values and color assignments.

The underlying LLM: Chart From Text automator in PluginBaseClasses/Chart.php was improved to use str_getcsv() instead of simple explode() for parsing CSV data, which properly handles quoted values containing delimiters. The prompt was also refined to explicitly request semicolon delimiters and explain quoting rules for text versus numeric values (fixing a typo from "paranthesis" to "parentheses" and "withe" to "with").

The diff includes extensive documentation in docs/modules/ai_automators/examples/chart-field.md with a complete setup guide covering field configuration, automator settings, and Field Widget Action attachment. The documentation was also reorganized to move text-to-audio and text-to-image examples into the examples directory and cross-reference them from multiple locations.

Timeline

  • Opened: January 9, 2026
  • First commit: February 11, 2026 (1 month later)
  • Last commit: February 16, 2026 (5 days later)

Collaboration

abhisekmazumdar tested the feature thoroughly, identified that chart data was being saved to the database but not immediately visible in the form (which is expected behavior for this implementation), and contributed documentation following the pattern from a related issue. a.dmitriiev requested documentation to help users understand how to configure such automators, which improved the usability of this feature. shalini_jha confirmed the testing findings to validate the behavior.

This summary is AI-generated and may contain errors. Check the original issue on Drupal.org for the full picture. For more digests, see Drupal Digests.

item.title: #3572309: Saving AI Agent via the visual editor doesn't work
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Major bug report
  • Version: 1.3.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Diff: 29cd1dc · +15/-22 · 1 file
  • Discussion: #3572309 · 6 contributors · 16 comments

Summary

A bug prevented AI Agent configuration changes from being saved when using the visual editor. When users edited tool settings in the sidebar panel and saved the workflow, their changes would not persist. Reloading the page would show the old values. This issue affected any modeler using the Modeler API to save AI Agents, making the visual editor unreliable for configuring agent behavior.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The bug was in Agent::addComponent() and Agent::addComponent() methods in the ai_agents module. The code hardcoded only specific behavioral settings like return_directly, require_usage, use_artifacts, and progress_message when mapping between the modeler component config and the agent's internal storage. Any other settings from contrib modules were silently dropped during save.

The component config contains two types of keys. Keys with triple underscores like url___action represent property restrictions stored in tool_usage_limits. Keys without triple underscores like description_override represent behavioral settings stored in tool_settings. The old code only saved the four hardcoded behavioral settings.

The fix changes the logic to split config generically based on whether keys contain triple underscores. Keys with ___ go to tool_usage_limits as property restrictions. Keys without ___ go to tool_settings as behavioral settings. This approach is future-proof because contrib modules can now add new behavioral settings without core code changes.

Timeline

  • Opened: February 9, 2026
  • Committed: February 16, 2026 (6 days later)

Collaboration

Ahmad Khader diagnosed the root cause in the ai_agents module and provided the fix that splits configuration keys generically based on the triple underscore pattern. d34dman tested the patch and confirmed that agent configuration now persists correctly after save. hrishikesh-dalal created the initial fork to address the issue.

This summary is AI-generated and may contain errors. Check the original issue on Drupal.org for the full picture. For more digests, see Drupal Digests.
